entities.name: FARMING SYSTEMS SUSTAINABILITY AND RESILIENCE (FSSR)
entities.description: A multidimensional framework developed to assess the sustainability and resilience of farming systems through five major domains and 115 indicators.

entities.name: SMALLHOLDER FARMING SYSTEMS
entities.description: Complex farm and food systems managed by small-scale farmers across different ecologies and cultures.

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: A sustainability domain within the FSSR framework consisting of 34 measurable indicators.

entities.name: ECONOMIC SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: A sustainability domain within the FSSR framework consisting of 29 measurable indicators.

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: A sustainability domain within the FSSR framework consisting of 12 measurable indicators.

entities.name: SOCIAL SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: A sustainability domain within the FSSR framework consisting of 25 measurable indicators.

entities.name: HUMAN WELL-BEING
entities.description: A sustainability domain within the FSSR framework consisting of 15 measurable indicators.

entities.name: SUSTAINABILITY INDEX
entities.description: An aggregated index with a maximum value of 100 used to represent the level of sustainability and resilience at different scales.

entities.name: SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: The capacity of a farming system to maintain its functions and services over time.

entities.name: RESILIENCE
entities.description: The capacity of a farming system to recover from shocks and stresses.

entities.name: VULNERABLE REGIONS
entities.description: Geographic areas where farming systems are susceptible to instability and require context-specific strategies for improvement.

entities.name: HOLISTIC SYSTEMS-ORIENTED APPROACH
entities.description: An approach recommended to address the intractable challenges of complex smallholder farm and food systems.
